mechanism_specificity_notes: Oral GSH largely degraded by gut peptidases; what reaches systemic circulation is broken into constituent amino acids. Negative or null RCTs are likely real for THIS formulation.
responder_population_notes: Cohort baseline; FRM-specific responder profile not established

mechanism_specificity_notes: Direct systemic delivery bypasses gut hydrolysis. Mechanistic case strong: replenishes intracellular GSH; reduces oxidative stress markers in vivo.
responder_population_notes: Pre-treatment low GSH/GSSG ratio + oxidative stress markers (8-OHdG, F2-isoprostanes elevated)

mechanism_specificity_notes: Phospholipid encapsulation protects GSH through stomach acid + pancreatic enzymes. Plasma GSH measurable post-dose. Bioavailability ~30-50% of IV.
responder_population_notes: Subset who tolerate oral but cannot access IV; pre-treatment low GSH/GSSG ratio

mechanism_specificity_notes: Transdermal absorption documented for small molecules; GSH penetration variable. Mechanistic hypothesis: skin GSH replenishment + slow systemic.
responder_population_notes: Sensitive children who cannot tolerate oral or IV; parents seeking lower-intervention option

mechanism_specificity_notes: Olfactory neuron uptake → potential CNS direct delivery bypassing BBB. Auro-GSH brand widely used; user reports of behavioral improvements within hours.
responder_population_notes: Children with measurable CNS oxidative stress (CSF lactate elevated; MRS NAA decreased); regression-phenotype subset

mechanism_specificity_notes: S-acetyl ester resistant to GI hydrolysis; deacetylated intracellularly. Shown to elevate intracellular GSH in cell models.
responder_population_notes: Cohort with intolerance to liposomal; subset with measurable post-dose GSH increase

mechanism_specificity_notes: Hardan 2012 Biol Psychiatry RCT (PMID 22342106) showed efficacy at 900-2700 mg/day in autism. Standard pharma form.
responder_population_notes: Modest oxidative stress signal; population-typical mild lactate; engine returns ~0.366 PHE-0002 loading

mechanism_specificity_notes: Approved for acetaminophen overdose; autism use is off-label. Higher peak plasma than oral.
responder_population_notes: Acute oxidative crisis; no established autism RCT for IV form

mechanism_specificity_notes: Respiratory mucolytic; minimal systemic absorption. Autism use anecdotal in subset with respiratory comorbidities.
responder_population_notes: Subset with chronic sinusitis or post-viral respiratory inflammation

mechanism_specificity_notes: Sublingual avoids first-pass; some children show measurable methionine increase. Inferior to injection for severe deficits.
responder_population_notes: Mild undermethylator with high adherence intolerance to injections

mechanism_specificity_notes: Hendren 2016 RCT (PMID 26889605) low-methionine subset showed 52% responder rate. Neubrander 64.5 mcg/kg every 3 days.
responder_population_notes: Walsh undermethylator phenotype: histamine_low + SAM/SAH_low; baseline plasma methionine <19 μM

mechanism_specificity_notes: Standard B12 deficiency treatment; less commonly used in autism than subcutaneous Neubrander protocol but pharmacokinetically equivalent.
responder_population_notes: Same as FRM-0011 but where IM is logistically preferred

mechanism_specificity_notes: Frye 2018 Mol Psychiatry RCT (PMID 27752075) FRAA-positive subgroup 78% responder rate. CALIBRATION ANCHOR: parent INT-0001 atlas signal must remain ≥80.
responder_population_notes: FRAA-blocking autoantibody positive (titer ≥1.0 ng/mL); cerebral folate deficiency phenotype

mechanism_specificity_notes: Bypasses MTHFR enzyme; preferred over folic acid for MTHFR C677T T/T.
responder_population_notes: MTHFR C677T T/T or C/T with elevated homocysteine; but contraindicated/cautioned in FRAA-positive (paradoxical worsening reported)

mechanism_specificity_notes: Synthetic monoglutamate; requires DHFR conversion. May worsen unmetabolized folic acid (UMFA) in poor methylators. PARADOXICAL WORSENING reported in FRAA-positive.
responder_population_notes: FRAA-positive: AVOID. MTHFR T/T: prefer 5-MTHF. General population: established for NTD prevention.

mechanism_specificity_notes: Standard turmeric powder bioavailability ~1%. Most negative RCTs of curcumin used this form.
responder_population_notes: Cohort baseline; very few clinical responders without enhanced absorption

mechanism_specificity_notes: 40-50× bioavailability vs standard turmeric. Liposomal encapsulation protects through GI tract.
responder_population_notes: Inflammatory subset (elevated IL-6/TNF); subset with measurable CRP reduction post-treatment

mechanism_specificity_notes: Phosphatidylcholine complex; 29× bioavail vs standard. Documented in arthritis and metabolic RCTs.
responder_population_notes: Inflammatory subset; tolerance to phosphatidylcholine

mechanism_specificity_notes: Standard L-carnitine; does NOT cross BBB efficiently. Best for systemic mitochondrial support.
responder_population_notes: Mitochondrial dysfunction phenotype with peripheral muscle/cardiac symptoms

mechanism_specificity_notes: Acetyl ester crosses BBB; preferred for cognitive/CNS effects. Multiple RCTs in cognitive disorders.
responder_population_notes: Mitochondrial dysfunction with CNS-dominant phenotype (cognitive/regression); pediatric mitochondrial disease

mechanism_specificity_notes: Cardiac/muscle preferred; less studied in autism specifically.
responder_population_notes: Subset with cardiac comorbidity (e.g., 22q11.2 deletion)

mechanism_specificity_notes: Compounded 1.5-4.5 mg from 50mg pharma tablets. Fjorback 2014 + multiple FM clinic case series.
responder_population_notes: PANS-overlap subset; chronic inflammation + autoimmune family history; treatment-resistant behavioral phenotype

mechanism_specificity_notes: Liquid form for children unable to swallow capsules; compounded.
responder_population_notes: Same as FRM-0022; pediatric patients with capsule intolerance

mechanism_specificity_notes: Transdermal compounded cream; lower absorption but anecdotal reports of efficacy in highly-sensitive children.
responder_population_notes: Severe sensory-sensitive children who cannot tolerate oral medication

mechanism_specificity_notes: Standard supplementation; multiple RCTs for deficiency correction. 25-OH-D target 30-50 ng/mL minimum.
responder_population_notes: Vitamin D deficient (25-OH-D <30); winter latitude; dark skin; obesity

mechanism_specificity_notes: Bypasses GI for malabsorbers; useful in IBD/cystic-fibrosis-overlap subset.
responder_population_notes: Children with malabsorption phenotype (chronic diarrhea, low vitamin levels despite supplementation)

mechanism_specificity_notes: Less potent + shorter duration than D3; mostly historical.
responder_population_notes: Generally not preferred; some prescribers use for vegan dietary requirements

formulation_name: Sulforaphane via broccoli sprout extract WITH myrosinase
mechanism_specificity_notes: Singh 2014 PNAS RCT (PMID 25313065) used GR-rich extract WITH active myrosinase. 9/26 = 35% responder rate vs 0% placebo.
responder_population_notes: Population-typical mild oxidative stress; no biomarker stratification in trial

formulation_name: Sulforaphane via broccoli sprout extract WITHOUT myrosinase
mechanism_specificity_notes: Without myrosinase, glucoraphanin → sulforaphane conversion depends on gut bacteria. Variable bioavailability.
responder_population_notes: Subset with healthy gut bacteria converts adequately; subset with dysbiosis (Bifido/Prevotella low) converts poorly

mechanism_specificity_notes: Used in Zimmerman 2021 (PMID 34034808) — younger children, weaker effects than Singh 2014. Standardized commercial product.
responder_population_notes: Children too young for raw sprouts; need standardized dosing

mechanism_specificity_notes: Standard quercetin poor bioavail (~2-5%). Most negative RCTs used this form.
responder_population_notes: Cohort baseline; rarely effective alone

mechanism_specificity_notes: 20× bioavail vs standard; documented mast-cell stabilization at this absorption level.
responder_population_notes: MCAS-overlap subset; elevated IL-6/TNF; allergic-like symptoms

mechanism_specificity_notes: Bromelain enzyme increases quercetin absorption + own anti-inflammatory effect.
responder_population_notes: MCAS or inflammation subset preferring enzyme support

mechanism_specificity_notes: Hsiao 2013 mouse model + clinical trials show specific strain-level effects. EVC001 strain reduces TLR/inflammatory markers.
responder_population_notes: GI cluster + microbiome dysbiosis (Bifido low at baseline); regressive immune-inflammatory subset

mechanism_specificity_notes: Buffington 2016 social behavior restoration in mouse models; single specific strain.
responder_population_notes: Social behavior phenotype; vagus-nerve modulation hypothesis

mechanism_specificity_notes: 8-strain blend; documented in IBS, IBD, ulcerative colitis. ASD use extrapolated.
responder_population_notes: GI cluster + dysbiosis; broad-spectrum support

mechanism_specificity_notes: Strain identity often poorly characterized; CFU at point of consumption variable; storage matters.
responder_population_notes: Cohort baseline; limited clinical evidence for unspecified strains

mechanism_specificity_notes: Lemonnier 2017 Transl Psychiatry RCT (PMID 28291262). 0.5-2.0 mg b.i.d. over 90 days. Standard pharma loop diuretic.
responder_population_notes: High-Cl⁻ subset (hypothesis); engine v0.4 PHE-0007 driver coverage

mechanism_specificity_notes: Compounded liquid for children unable to swallow tablets.
responder_population_notes: Pediatric administration; same population as FRM-0038

mechanism_specificity_notes: Hypothesized direct CNS delivery bypassing systemic diuresis. Research/anecdotal.
responder_population_notes: Subset where systemic diuretic side-effects (electrolyte derangement) preclude oral

mechanism_specificity_notes: Wright 2011 J Autism Dev Disord (PMID 20535539). 2-10 mg titrated. Sleep latency reduction validated.
responder_population_notes: Severe dysomnia not amenable to behavioral management; sleep-axis phenotype

mechanism_specificity_notes: Slow-release for sleep maintenance vs sleep onset. Wasdell 2008 documented benefit for sleep maintenance.
responder_population_notes: Sleep maintenance phenotype (frequent night wakenings) rather than sleep onset

mechanism_specificity_notes: Faster onset than oral; useful for severe sleep onset insomnia.
responder_population_notes: Severe sleep onset phenotype; older children/adolescents

mechanism_specificity_notes: Poor systemic absorption; works locally on gut mast cells. Useful for MCAS-GI overlap.
responder_population_notes: MCAS subset with prominent GI symptoms; food-mediated reactivity

mechanism_specificity_notes: Allergic rhinitis treatment; off-label for MCAS-respiratory.
responder_population_notes: MCAS subset with respiratory/airway symptoms

mechanism_specificity_notes: Asthma treatment; off-label for chronic respiratory MCAS.
responder_population_notes: MCAS-asthma overlap; chronic respiratory inflammation

mechanism_specificity_notes: Chez 2002 J Child Neurol (PMID 12585724) used 800 mg/day L-carnosine. GABA modulation via homocarnosine; frontal/temporal cortex protection.
responder_population_notes: GABA-axis subset; subclinical epileptiform EEG; valproic acid concomitant

mechanism_specificity_notes: Ophthalmologic use for cataracts; minimal systemic absorption. NOT for autism systemic effects.
responder_population_notes: Ophthalmologic comorbidity only

mechanism_specificity_notes: Chelated form; high bioavailability; well-tolerated; minimal GI side effects.
responder_population_notes: Mg-deficient subset; sleep + anxiety phenotype

mechanism_specificity_notes: Crosses BBB; brain-targeted Mg form. Documented hippocampal Mg increase in animal models.
responder_population_notes: Cognitive/memory phenotype; preferred for CNS-dominant Mg deficiency

mechanism_specificity_notes: Poor bioavailability (~4-10%); useful as laxative more than Mg supplementation.
responder_population_notes: GI cluster: useful for constipation but inadequate Mg repletion

mechanism_specificity_notes: Transdermal absorption documented but variable. Useful for children with severe oral intolerance.
responder_population_notes: Severe oral intolerance; sensory-sensitive children
